_comment: Canonical msgpack wire contract for the 10 replication-DDL BatchOp variants. Generated by shamir-query-builder builders, serialized with rmp_serde::to_vec_named (named-field maps). Consumed by the Rust regression test (tests/repl_ddl_msgpack.rs) and the TypeScript client parity test in #376.
_key_order_note: msgpack maps produced by rmp_serde::to_vec_named serialize fields in STRUCT DECLARATION ORDER (the order the fields appear in the Rust struct definition), NOT alphabetically. TypeScript parity code MUST emit keys in this same declaration order to reproduce these exact bytes. Map keys are unique per op (the discriminator key is the first-named field of each op struct), so the server's key-based dispatch in BatchOp::deserialize does not depend on order — but byte-exact reproduction across languages does.

_value_notes.read_only_flags: list_publications/list_subscriptions/replication_status serialize the boolean discriminator as msgpack true (0xc3); the false-value is skip_serializing_if'd away (see repl_ops::is_false), so a default-constructed payload is an empty map — the fixture always uses the builder which sets true.
_value_notes.alter_subscription: Only the SetProfile SubAction variant is pinned here (it is the sole payload-carrying variant). Pause/Resume are snake_case unit strings already pinned by repl_nested_enums_wire_shape in shamir-query-types.
_value_notes.repl_scope: Optional repo/table fields are skip_serializing_if = Option::is_none, so a db-only scope emits just {"db": ...}; the fixture's create_publication scope is db-only.
